svg.title: AWS VPC architecture — Mumbai and Delhi with subnets, IGW, NAT, peering, and SSH bastion
svg.desc: Structural diagram showing two VPCs (Mumbai 10.0.0.0/16 and Delhi 172.16.0.0/16) each with public and private subnets, internet gateways, NAT gateway, EC2 instances, and a VPC peering connection between them.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does internet traffic enter a VPC through an IGW?')
sendPrompt('What is an Internet Gateway and how does it work?')
sendPrompt('What is an Internet Gateway and how does it work?')
sendPrompt('How does a NAT Gateway allow private EC2 instances to reach the internet?')
sendPrompt('What is a bastion host and how do I SSH through it?')
sendPrompt('How do I SSH into a private EC2 instance through a bastion host?')
sendPrompt('What routes does the public route table need?')
sendPrompt('What routes does the private route table need?')
sendPrompt('How do I launch an EC2 instance in the Delhi VPC public subnet?')
sendPrompt('How does peering allow private EC2s in Delhi to talk to Mumbai?')
sendPrompt('What routes does the Delhi public route table need?')
sendPrompt('What peering routes do I add to the Delhi route table?')
sendPrompt('How does VPC peering work and what are its limits?')
sendPrompt('What peering routes do I add to the Mumbai route table?')
sendPrompt('How do I accept a VPC peering connection request?')
sendPrompt('How do I convert a PEM key to PPK using PuTTYgen?')
sendPrompt('How do I configure SSH agent forwarding through a bastion host?')
sendPrompt('What Security Group rules does a private EC2 need for SSH access?')
